what: Use bf16 Q with fp8 KV (a16w8 kernel) to skip Q quantization overhead
why: Q quantization (per_tensor_quant_hip) costs ~5-10us per call. The a16w8 MLA kernel accepts bf16 Q directly with fp8 KV. This saves quant cost while keeping fp8 KV bandwidth savings.

what: Direct ASM kernel calls with fully pre-allocated buffers for non-persistent fp8 path, bypassing mla_decode_fwd Python overhead
why: mla_decode_fwd allocates logits, attn_lse, final_lse tensors on EVERY call. For splits==1+fp8, logits IS the output (no reduce). Bypassing mla_decode_fwd and calling mla_decode_stage1_asm_fwd directly with pre-allocated buffers eliminates per-call allocations (~5-10us). Also skips per_tensor_quant_hip import try/except overhead.

what: Use mla_decode_fwd non-persistent for ALL shapes with pre-allocated output, avoiding persistent metadata overhead
why: Persistent mode requires metadata build (get_mla_metadata_v1) and 2-phase reduce. Non-persistent mode auto-selects splits and uses Triton stage2 reduce. For small shapes (bs=4-64), avoiding persistent metadata and using auto-split should reduce overhead. Pre-allocate output buffer to avoid allocation.

what: Use bf16 Q + bf16 KV (a16w16) via mla_decode_fwd to eliminate Q quantization overhead
why: Per-tensor Q quantization (per_tensor_quant_hip) costs ~5-10us per call, which is significant for small shapes. The a16w16 kernel (bf16 Q + bf16 KV) exists for non-persistent mode with qseqlen=1 (mla_dec_stage1_bf16_a16w16_subQ16_mqa16). Trading 2x KV bandwidth for eliminated quant overhead may net positive for small batch shapes.
